fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Pennsylvania Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity pennsylvania, how to transfer dietitian license to pennsylvania, dietitian compact pennsylvania, pennsylvania dietitian endorsement application

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian-Nutritionist (LDN)
license_types.description: The standard credential for practicing dietetics or serving as a nutritionist in Pennsylvania. Administered through the State Board of Nursing.

steps.title: Complete Education and Supervised Practice
steps.description: Complete a didactic program in dietetics and supervised practice experiences accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND).

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians
steps.description: Successfully pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Apply for a Dietitian License
steps.description: Submit an application for licensure to the Pennsylvania State Board of Nursing along with required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Must be sent directly from your degree-granting university to the State Board of Nursing.
Verification of a passing score on the CDR or BCNS exam, sent directly from the testing agency.
A certificate proving completion of the mandatory 3-hour Pennsylvania child abuse recognition and reporting course.

provisional_text: Pennsylvania does not grant temporary permits, but Act 41 allows the Board to issue provisional licenses to practitioners coming from other jurisdictions who need time to fulfill specific PA requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pennsylvania does not currently participate in interstate licensure compacts for nutritionists. Out-of-state applicants must apply via endorsement through the PALS system and provide verification of their active, good-standing license from their home state, along with proof of passing a Board-approved exam.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to get an LDN license in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee for a Licensed Dietitian-Nutritionist in Pennsylvania is $95. After issuance, it costs $71 to renew the license biennially.

faqs.question: Which Pennsylvania board issues the dietitian license?
faqs.answer: Unlike many states with dedicated dietetics boards, Pennsylvania regulates Licensed Dietitian-Nutritionists under the State Board of Nursing within the broader Department of State.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Pennsylvania dietitians?
faqs.answer: To renew biennially, you must complete 30 hours of continuing professional education. Of those 30 hours, 2 hours must strictly be a Board-approved course in child abuse recognition and reporting.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Pennsylvania does not currently participate in interstate licensure compacts for nutritionists. Out-of-state applicants must apply via endorsement through the PALS system and provide verification of their active, good-standing license from their home state, along with proof of passing a Board-approved exam.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Pennsylvania
hero.dek: Pennsylvania Dietitian License requires completion of an accredited education program, passing the Registration Examination for Dietitians, and completing supervised practice hours.
